title: Traffic And Undercut/Overcut Opportunity Features
summary: Official F1 strategy explainers emphasize that pit timing depends on undercut range, overcut viability, pit-window timing, and traffic gaps, not just own tire age.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explainers emphasize that pit timing depends on undercut range, overcut viability, pit-window timing, and traffic gaps, not just own tire age. The current feature set has same-lap context, but not explicit rejoin-window or rival-pressure proxies.
implementation_hint: Within each lap field, engineer features such as nearest-car pressure, count of rivals on older tires nearby, free-air rejoin slot proxy, boxed-in flag, teammate shadow, and whether staying out increases overcut risk. Use Position, same-lap ranks, LapTime_Delta, and race-progress bins as gap proxies.
expected_effect: Improves ambiguous mid-race decisions where multiple drivers have similar wear but only some have a strategically viable stop next lap.
risk: Without actual interval gaps, these are indirect proxies and may behave differently across circuits with unusual warmup or overtaking profiles.
